methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Theseus is a Seed-stage company in Defense & Security founded by Carl Schoeller (CEO), Ian Laffey, and Sacha Lévy. The company has raised $4.3M to date. The Defense & Security sector includes 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

Valuation analysis relies on a Stage-Progression methodology, as peer-median comparison is not applicable given insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage Defense & Security peers meeting minimum sample thresholds. The Stage-Progression method applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression to the $4.3M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $10.8M.

The triangulated analysis supports a median valuation of $11M, with a range of $9M to $12M. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single methodological approach. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer valuation data at the same stage and tier, product differentiation relative to the broader Defense & Security landscape, and the typical post-Seed valuation trajectory for this sector. A fuller analysis would benefit from traction metrics, revenue run-rate (if any), or customer commitments to ground the estimate.
